questions.text: Give a general description and introduction to your organization.
State the start and end date of the year for which you are reporting data.
Select the countries/areas in which you operate.
Select the currency used for all financial information disclosed throughout your response.
Does your organization have an ISIN code or another unique identifier (e.g., Ticker, CUSIP, etc.)?
Did you have an emissions target that was active in the reporting year?
questions.gapHint: A formally adopted emissions reduction target (e.g., 'reduce Scope 1+2 emissions by X% by year Y'). Often set with help from a sustainability consultant or SBTi sector guidance.

questions.text: Explain why you did not have an emissions target, and forecast how your emissions will change over the next five years.
Did you have emissions reduction initiatives that were active within the reporting year?
questions.gapHint: Specific projects like LED retrofits, renewable energy switching, or efficiency upgrades with tracked CO2e savings.

questions.text: Identify the total number of initiatives at each stage of development, and the estimated CO2e savings.
Provide details on the initiatives implemented in the reporting year.
Why did you not have any emissions reduction initiatives active during the reporting year?
Provide your base year and base year emissions.
Select the name of the standard, protocol, or methodology you have used to collect activity data and calculate emissions.
What were your organization's gross global Scope 1 emissions in metric tons CO2e?
Describe your organization's approach to reporting Scope 2 emissions.
questions.defaultAnswer: Location-based method, using grid average emission factors for the operating region.

questions.text: What were your organization's gross global Scope 2 emissions in metric tons CO2e?
Account for your organization's gross global Scope 3 emissions, disclosing and explaining any exclusions.
questions.gapHint: Scope 3 (supply chain) emissions data — typically estimated from spend-based or supplier-specific data. Most SMEs starting out exclude Scope 3 in year one and disclose that exclusion explicitly.

questions.text: Describe your gross global combined Scope 1 and 2 emissions for the reporting year in metric tons CO2e per unit currency total revenue.
Break down your total gross global Scope 1 emissions by country/region.
questions.gapHint: Only required if you operate in more than one country/region.

questions.text: Break down your total gross global Scope 2 emissions by country/region.
questions.gapHint: Only required if you operate in more than one country/region.

questions.text: Is your organization able to break down your emission data for any subsidiaries included in your CDP response?
What percentage of your total operational spend in the reporting year was on energy?
Select which energy-related activities your organization has undertaken.
Report your organization's energy consumption totals (excluding feedstocks) in MWh.
questions.validationNote: Renewable + non-renewable MWh must equal total energy consumption MWh.

questions.text: Indicate the verification/assurance status that applies to your reported emissions.
questions.defaultAnswer: No third-party assurance or verification obtained for the reporting year.

questions.text: Do you engage with your value chain on climate-related issues?
Provide details of your climate-related supplier engagement strategy.
questions.gapHint: A documented approach to discussing emissions/climate practices with key suppliers. Often doesn't exist yet for early-stage SME reporting.

questions.text: Provide details for the person that has signed off (approved) your CDP climate change response.
What is your company's annual revenue for the stated reporting period?
Allocate your emissions to your customers listed below according to the goods or services you have sold them in this reporting period.
Please propose any mutually beneficial climate-related projects you could collaborate on with specific CDP Supply Chain members.
Have requests or initiatives by CDP Supply Chain members prompted your organization to undertake organizational-level emissions reduction initiatives?
